purpose: Determines whether global aging resolves as net inflationary or deflationary — the single most consequential unresolvable uncertainty for all cross-asset positioning.
components: Supply-side channel: labor scarcity → ULC exceeding productivity → inflationary pressure
Demand-side channel: reduced consumption in old age → deflationary pressure
Political economy channel: fiscal expansion under supply constraint → maximum inflationary pressure
Japan precedent: 27 years below-target inflation during extreme aging → deflationary evidence
Confounding filter: 2022-2025 data contaminated by supply chains, fiscal stimulus, Ukraine
current_assessment: Genuinely ~50/50 with 6/8 agents converging. macro_strategist_01 60/40 inflationary lean challenged as overreach given Japan precedent. Resolution timeline 3-5+ years. Premature closure is the costliest error.
limitations: N<3 pure old-age-dependency inflationary episodes globally
2022-2025 data window massively confounded and cannot be used to update priors
Japan is the highest-N single case but institutional uniqueness limits generalizability
The binary framing may itself be wrong — mixed or regime-switching outcomes are possible

purpose: Analyzes how sovereign debt can be reduced under demographic constraints by decomposing the 1946-1980 template into constituent channels and assessing impairment of each.
components: Inflation channel (~40% of historical deleveraging) — available but politically costly
Growth channel (~35%, of which only ~40% is labor force; rest is TFP) — impaired but less than commonly assumed
Primary surplus channel (~15%) — blocked by 14-15% GDP entitlement spending vs 3-4% in 1945
Financial repression channel (~10%) — 0/3 success rate without growth tailwinds
current_assessment: Template impaired but constraint overstated by ~2x per Solow decomposition. TFP substitution for labor growth is key unknown. AI wildcard could significantly loosen. Entitlement spending blocks surpluses unconditionally.
limitations: TFP may itself be demographically influenced through innovation and dynamism channels
Financial repression without growth: 0/3 historical success
AI TFP acceleration is speculative and untested at macro scale
No historical template for deleveraging under simultaneous global aging

purpose: Evaluates when Japan's demographic-financial experience can and cannot be used as a template for other economies, preventing the single-analogue fallacy.
components: Five structural uniqueness tests (NIIP, domestic ownership, CB ownership, CA surplus, deflationary backdrop)
Political economy sequence generalization (denial → accommodation → monetization → coordination)
Asymmetric usage audit: is Japan cited selectively for negative vs positive outcomes?
Regime break assessment: 2023-2025 Japan outperformance as live falsification test
current_assessment: Financial outcomes non-generalizable (US fails all 5 structural tests). Political economy sequence may generalize but small sample. 2023-2025 too confounded for conclusions. Need 5+ years sustained performance to revise consensus.
limitations: Single case study with unique institutional features
2023-2025 confounded by yen depreciation, governance reforms, BoJ policy shift
Asymmetric usage confirmed — Japan used cautionary for debt but dismissed for positive outcomes
40% dependency ratio threshold as political economy trigger is not empirically established

purpose: Identifies the current stock-bond correlation regime and assesses persistence, with direct implications for portfolio construction, hedging, and term premium.
components: CPI level threshold: >4% → 85% positive correlation
Four sustaining conditions for negative regime (demographics, globalization, disinflation, EM reserves) — all weakening
NK micro-foundation: supply-shock dominance → positive correlation
Factor evidence: value premium co-movement with correlation regime
Persistence statistics: positive regime >5 years has only 25-40% probability
current_assessment: Positive regime likely (~60% probability) with all four negative-regime conditions weakening. But persistence >5 years only 25-40% probable, tempering duration conviction but not directional.
limitations: Persistence probability estimates have wide confidence intervals
Conditioned on inflation-deflation resolution (~50/50) which caps overall confidence
Reflexive loop between correlation, hedging, and term premium complicates static analysis
Historical regimes involved different structural contexts than current

purpose: Assesses EM country vulnerability through the interaction of demographic structure, food dependency, remittance dependency, and carry trade dynamics.
components: Youth-bulge carry vs tail risk (food CPI weight, informal sector size, instability precedent)
Remittance dependency mapping (% GDP, source country immigration policy trajectory)
Food price → instability transmission chain (Arab Spring, Egypt 2022-23 templates)
Fertilizer supply concentration risk (Russia/Belarus potash, Morocco phosphate)
Reserve adequacy under demographic stress (healthcare imports, food imports)
current_assessment: Youth-bulge EMs offer carry but sovereign CDS systematically underweights demographic-instability tail. Remittance compression is most underappreciated channel. Food price transmission is most immediate risk.
limitations: Sovereign CDS demographic recalibration methodology not established
Reserve adequacy adjustment is conceptual only — no empirical validation
Country-specific analysis needed (Nigeria, Egypt, Pakistan, Philippines have very different structures)
OECD immigration policy itself uncertain — restriction gap thesis untested in recession

purpose: Evaluates how demographic labor scarcity structurally impairs the credit cycle's natural correction mechanisms, focused on the 2026-2029 maturity wall.
components: Historical labor loosening valve: present in 4/4 modern default cycles as margin relief
Labor force growth constraint: 0.3% vs historical 0.8-1.2%
Covenant structure degradation: 87% covenant-lite, PIK toggle rising
EBITDA addback assumptions: 25-30% calibrated to higher labor growth
Unfalsifiability test: what observation could disconfirm the thesis?
current_assessment: Direction validated across 5+ agents but magnitude unknowable (N=0). Thesis circulated since 2023 without observable stress. Unfalsifiability is a valid methodological concern. This is a directional thesis, not an actionable trade.
limitations: Magnitude genuinely unknowable with N=0 for this specific combination
Unfalsifiability: every non-confirming observation can be dismissed as premature
Thesis circulated since 2023 without observable credit stress
The 'most actionable trade' label was definitively refuted — direction only
